123660.explanation: Residents deserve influence in major decisions. We choose strong citizen participation and dialogue, not a polarizing yes/no referendum with winners and losers.

123663.explanation: Farmers get space for secondary activities such as shops or campsites. Condition: fitting in the environment, traffic, and nature, and clearly subordinate to the agricultural business.

123664.explanation: We support local energy cooperatives if financially responsible and residents directly benefit. Think of appropriate subsidies/loans and assistance with permits and organization.

123665.explanation: We protect space and livability: no unwanted large-scale distribution centers that burden the landscape, traffic, and safety. We prefer to strengthen the economy via local SME opportunities.

123667.explanation: Housing belongs to business operations. The principle is on own premises with strict standards and supervision; where that is really not possible, only via tailored solutions without nuisance for neighbors.

123670.explanation: No ban on terrace heating, but yes to sustainability. We encourage smart, efficient alternatives (such as IR) and make agreements to limit unnecessary energy use.

123672.explanation: Free bulky waste collection is not a good idea. We want an affordable, clear, and service-oriented system that stimulates reuse and prevents dumping.

123674.explanation: Accessibility is fundamental. We help entrepreneurs with advice, co-financing, and making use of subsidies so that buildings become more accessible for people with disabilities.

123675.explanation: Space for a company house or necessary housing at the business location is possible, but no fragmentation of the rural area. Only under strict conditions and with spatial quality.

123677.explanation: Extra support is possible but only with demonstrable local added value. Transparency, performance agreements, and periodic evaluation are conditions for municipal contributions.

123679.explanation: Music education is valuable. We encourage it through cooperation with schools and associations and targeted subsidies where needed, without a one-size-fits-all obligation.

123682.explanation: More focus on enforcement where needed. Enforcement officers (BOAs) strengthen safety and livability by visible supervision of nuisance, traffic, and undermining in all centers.

123683.explanation: Camera surveillance only where really needed. We work with clear goals, privacy safeguards, limited deployment at hotspots, and fixed evaluation of its effectiveness.

123688.explanation: Holiday parks are for recreation. Permanent residence is solved with real homes in the centers, not via loopholes. Enforce where necessary, tailor where policy allows.

123689.explanation: We protect the open landscape but do not block everything. Compact, logical expansions ('one street added') are possible if the need is demonstrable and the village benefits from it.

123690.explanation: A reciprocal obligation is possible, but always appropriate and humane. We take into account health, caregiving, and reintegration opportunities; the goal is participation, not punishment.

123692.explanation: We look beyond a single strict income threshold. Assistance must align with the real situation; where necessary, we broaden criteria and work with tailored solutions to prevent poverty.

123694.explanation: We expand charging points where there is demand and the electricity grid can handle it. Smart planning and fair distribution, so it doesn't become symbolic policy and parking remains manageable.

123695.explanation: 30 km/h zones where it demonstrably becomes safer and more livable, such as school zones and busy streets. Not a standard for every road; we choose well-founded tailor-made solutions.

123696.explanation: Freedom of choice is important, but within what is available and affordable. We guarantee quality and accessibility and, where possible, make room for appropriate care based on faith or culture.

123699.explanation: We abolish dog tax. It's outdated and unfair. Municipal costs are covered transparently from general funds.

123701.explanation: More business parks are possible, but only for genuine economic need. Conditions include good access and integration; growth must contribute to jobs and livability.

123703.explanation: No priority based on status. Scarce social rental housing goes to local applicants and urgent cases; fair, transparent, and within the rules.

123705.explanation: Solar panels on roofs first. Only on agricultural land under strict conditions and with landscape integration; we prevent fragmentation and protect valuable agricultural areas.

123709.explanation: Regional language is culture. Attention to Zeelandic-Flemish can be accessible through projects and guest lessons, without burdening schools extra or overloading the curriculum.

123710.explanation: No coffeeshop. We choose safety and livability and do not want extra attraction or enforcement pressure in our centers.

123712.explanation: No expansion to loose room rental as a standard. It does not help the housing market and can cause nuisance; we choose real homes and strong enforcement against abuses.

123715.explanation: No fully car-free center as a dogma. We work on a more attractive and safer center with car-reduced measures but keep access for residents, loading/unloading, and emergency services.

123718.explanation: We aim for a healthy mix of owner-occupied and rental housing, but a fixed percentage must fit demand, location, and feasibility; quality and affordability come first.

123719.explanation: Yes, growth is needed to absorb aging and maintain facilities. We utilize our international position and build appropriately so new residents contribute to livability and the economy.

123660.explanation: That is what an elected municipal council is for.

123663.explanation: New economic drivers are good for this sector.

123664.explanation: Every initiative to generate more sustainable energy should be embraced.

123665.explanation: Yes, on special industrial sites. This fits within the Economic agenda of the municipality of Sluis. But not everywhere free rein to realize this.

123667.explanation: This way there is better insight into living conditions.

123670.explanation: This is a free choice for an entrepreneur. Banning gas is not necessary because an entrepreneur can make that assessment themselves.

123672.explanation: Collecting bulky waste for free once a month prevents littering.

123674.explanation: This is a (legal) responsibility of an entrepreneur. Financial support does not belong here.

123675.explanation: This is possible, but not always and everywhere. A good consideration is important.

123677.explanation: This is important information for local residents.

123679.explanation: Music fits all atmospheres in your life.

123682.explanation: The municipality of Sluis already has enough enforcement officers (BOAs). Gentle coaching could be beneficial.

123683.explanation: Preventive supervision promotes safety and the feeling of security. In the past, we often dealt with ram raids and robberies.

123688.explanation: There are enough holiday homes in the centers that can be used as a permanent residence. This should not happen in holiday parks. Holiday homes in parks are holiday homes...

123689.explanation: Build, build, and build. Much is already possible on the edges of existing village boundaries. But building outside those boundaries should also be allowed.

123690.explanation: This is very important. It boosts self-esteem and thus keeps them part of society.

123692.explanation: These people must be helped. Financially, this is also possible in Sluis.

123694.explanation: There are already many charging points in Sluis, but the electric car market is growing and the charging stations must grow with it.

123695.explanation: Safety above all, especially in built-up areas.

123696.explanation: That is possible, but where do you draw the line?

123699.explanation: This has been a long-running discussion during election time. But it needs to be addressed once and for all.

123701.explanation: Is one of the points from the Economic agenda.

123703.explanation: No, our residents have these rights as well. A status holder must also wait, just like our residents. They cannot, barring exceptions, receive preferential treatment to the detriment of our residents.

123705.explanation: This can be done on designated plots. We need alternative energy sources for the future. The location and visibility determine where this is responsible.

123709.explanation: I think our children already speak the dialect very well. Extra attention is not necessary. Educational attention about our dialect would be beneficial, so they learn more about the region.

123712.explanation: That's possible, but only with a good and appropriate permit to ensure safety.

123715.explanation: Make it car-reduced, but not car-free!

123718.explanation: Build what the market demands. A project developer knows very well what needs to be built.

123719.explanation: The new council must really focus on growth in the number of inhabitants. The VVD aims to grow to 30,000 inhabitants. And that is a considerable challenge.

123660.explanation: We find the opinions of residents certainly important, but see the referendum tool as less suitable.

123663.explanation: We are generous in supporting farmers with new economic drivers. But ALWAYS goes too far for us; it also depends on the type of business, location, etc.

123664.explanation: We are in favor of cooperatives of residents to generate sustainable energy. We would like residents to (co)benefit from this instead of only the large energy companies.

123665.explanation: We want economic developments with added value for our residents. Distribution centers especially take up a lot of space.

123667.explanation: Hiring employers are responsible and in our view this does not necessarily have to be on their own premises. There may be other possibilities as well.

123672.explanation: That is already the case now.

123674.explanation: Accessibility is certainly important. The municipality is responsible for municipal buildings. Entrepreneurs are responsible for their own buildings.

123675.explanation: Opposed to the hardening (paving over) of the rural area.

123677.explanation: We find the local broadcaster important. They must, of course, demonstrate how much money is needed.

123679.explanation: It's more nuanced: music lessons are important for every child. But the municipality does not have to subsidize everything.

123682.explanation: Enforcement to ensure safety is, of course, important, but does that require extra enforcement officers?

123683.explanation: At this moment, in our opinion, there is insufficient reason for that.

123688.explanation: They should not be mixed. Holiday parks are for holidays, residential centers are for living!

123689.explanation: Initially, we must build within existing village boundaries. But if more is needed, a new neighborhood may have to be built outside.

123690.explanation: The basis is that everyone should work. But there are situations where that is not possible; therefore, customization must be possible.

123692.explanation: The income limit should be set so that there is no immediate poverty trap when someone starts working. The minimum policy in the municipality of Sluis is good and should remain so.

123694.explanation: There must be sufficient charging points in municipal parking lots. That does not mean we should fill all streets with charging stations.

123696.explanation: Good care must be accessible, affordable, and nearby for everyone.

123701.explanation: Business activity in industrial areas is important. But they must be real companies with added value.

123703.explanation: There must be sufficient social rental homes for everyone, so no one has to wait long for a house. This problem does not currently occur in the municipality of Sluis.

123705.explanation: Solar panels on farmland are not our first option. First on roofs.

123709.explanation: Dialect is not a task for schools.

123710.explanation: If an application is submitted, it must be possible.

123712.explanation: Tourist rental in residential centers is opposed in any case. It must be for living purposes. Regarding labor migrants, it may be necessary to rent out rooms?

123719.explanation: Yes, limited growth to maintain facilities and thus livability.

123660.explanation: As CDA, we are not in favor of a binding referendum. Our council members are easily accessible.

123664.explanation: The municipality must facilitate and possibly provide a one-time investment contribution.

123665.explanation: Distribution centers offer future prospects and employment, but we should not build for vacancy.

123667.explanation: Provided the housing complies with laws and regulations.

123670.explanation: Our preference is for electric terrace heating.

123672.explanation: Continuation of current policy; we want to prevent illegal dumping.

123674.explanation: The municipality actively participates in improving accessibility.

123675.explanation: Building a house must be possible, provided the regulations are met.

123677.explanation: We recognize the importance of the local broadcaster and want to continue supporting it.

123679.explanation: We believe it is important that our young people come into contact with music at an early age.

123682.explanation: Where necessary, we deploy BOAs (Special Enforcement Officers) specifically.

123683.explanation: Camera surveillance alone does not provide safety, but if there is a demand for it, we want to facilitate it.

123688.explanation: Where the zoning plan allows, it must be possible.

123689.explanation: Building on the edge of the village center should be possible.

123690.explanation: We also see volunteer work as socially meaningful work.

123692.explanation: The current policy is already at 130%, and we want to continue this.

123694.explanation: Taking into account the distribution of the number of charging points.

123695.explanation: Traffic safety is determined by logical and safe road design and not just by lower speed limits.

123696.explanation: Having a choice should be possible, provided it can reasonably be offered.

123699.explanation: We find the current service offered (e.g., dog waste bags) important. This is currently paid for through this tax.

123701.explanation: The expansion of existing sites should be possible.

123703.explanation: National laws and regulations are leading.

123705.explanation: As the CDA, we follow the solar ladder, solar panels on roofs first before covering agricultural land.

123709.explanation: Each community has its own dialect; we consider it the parents' task to pay attention to this.

123710.explanation: No coffeeshops in the municipality of Sluis.

123712.explanation: But only if it complies with laws and regulations.

123715.explanation: This makes it more difficult for people with disabilities to access the center.

123718.explanation: According to national laws and regulations, namely 30%.

123719.explanation: More residents promote the livability of the communities.

123660.explanation: Fully agree. On important issues, residents should be able to express their opinion through a binding referendum.

123663.explanation: We should not waste too much agricultural land by adding a campsite to a farm; existing farm campsites may expand up to a maximum of 35 units (only in combination with a farm). No new campsites, but a sales point for own produce is allowed.

123664.explanation: Making homes more sustainable is expensive; permanent residents should be able to apply for subsidies for this, both owners and tenants. However, there should be a limit set on the property tax (WOZ) value. No subsidy above a certain amount.

123665.explanation: If the centers are small-scale, fitting within our municipal industrial areas, no large massive distribution centers should be built.

123667.explanation: 'Must' is a strong word. If those employers have the space and facilities, that is fine; otherwise, they should accommodate employees in a good location.

123670.explanation: Preferably no terrace heating, but 'must' is a strong word. The SP is not in favor of this kind of heating.

123672.explanation: Monthly free bulky waste collection is simply necessary for the SP. Charging for collection will lead to much more illegal dumping. It also creates a barrier for low-income residents in our municipality. It should simply be free.

123674.explanation: Entrepreneurs in the municipality of Sluis are socially responsible and capable enough to adapt their buildings so everyone, including people with disabilities, can access their business.

123675.explanation: There are rules for this; if every farmer builds one or more houses on their land, you get uncontrolled proliferation that cannot be managed. Under certain conditions, this could be allowed but only for social housing and starter homes.

123677.explanation: For local news, this channel is necessary; it must be independent from sponsorship funds to broadcast news impartially. No outside interference.

123679.explanation: If there is interest or interest is generated, certainly. Think of appointing one music teacher for all primary schools.

123682.explanation: BOAs are the eyes and ears of our municipality and should be deployed at events and for maintaining safety. The municipality of Sluis has too few.

123683.explanation: There are few locations in our municipality where criminal activities take place. Our municipality is large (many villages); it is impossible to install cameras everywhere, and privacy laws also play a role.

123688.explanation: The SP finds this position worthless. Holiday homes are for tourists and those houses do not belong in our communities. Only our permanent residents registered with the municipality can have a permanent address.

123689.explanation: This is a difficult position. Yes, if it is possible according to planning rules and only for permanent residence, not for second homes, not for tourism.

123690.explanation: If people are on welfare and must do work, then the work should be paid and they would no longer receive benefits. Otherwise, this could be seen as labor displacement, which the SP is absolutely against.

123692.explanation: Agreed, the SP wants to raise that threshold to 130% of the minimum income level.

123694.explanation: If spots are needed, yes. Residents can indicate this to the municipality. Neighborhoods where there are almost (or not yet) any electric cars should not have them placed. Parking spaces are scarce in some neighborhoods.

123695.explanation: Within built-up areas, this is a justified choice; for the safety of other road users, this is a must.

123696.explanation: If these caregivers are available, then yes; if not, care must be provided by others. Care should be based on religious conviction.

123699.explanation: Yes, directly, it is simply a cash cow for the municipality. It is an old tax from the time of horse-drawn carts. So, abolish it or alternatively impose an extra tourist tax on pets.

123701.explanation: If more sites are needed for small-scale industry, then yes; but developing land and then leaving it fallow for years serves no purpose.

123703.explanation: The number of status holders is determined by the national government; the municipality must house them, but priority should be given to assigning housing to our (future) residents as well.

123705.explanation: Agricultural land is our greatest asset and important for food production. There are several other locations suitable for solar panels, such as above parking lots, which also provide the added benefit of shading vehicles.

123709.explanation: Yes, the SP also believes the dialect is important for our municipality; after all, it is a heritage.

123710.explanation: As long as dealings in soft drugs are illegal, we prefer no coffee shops.

123712.explanation: No, this is an uncontrollable phenomenon; it already happens that houses are bought up and many people working in the relevant area stay there. They are simply exploited.

123715.explanation: Yes, on busy days, even after 11:00 AM is fine. We want to close off the center then with movable bollards (operated by a public official).

123718.explanation: Fully agree, the eight-lists in some areas should be eliminated; the percentage may even be allowed to be somewhat higher in our opinion.

123719.explanation: Yes, also agree, but work must be done to create jobs, not only in tourism but also in other sectors, SMEs and small-scale industry.

123664.explanation: New initiatives must be financially self-sufficient. Note: List Babijn does not want horizon pollution from wind turbines and also opposes solar panels on fertile agricultural land.

123667.explanation: Seasonal workers meet a need and keep our economy running. Housing must be strictly regulated to prevent nuisance.

123672.explanation: This is to prevent illegal dumping.

123674.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in our society.

123677.explanation: The contribution from the national government must be used for this purpose.

123682.explanation: Safety on the street and in general must be guaranteed at all times, as it is also fundamental to a healthy living and business environment in our beautiful municipality of Sluis.

123683.explanation: Entrepreneurs in the center of Sluis have been trying to get this off the ground for a long time and are even willing to finance it independently. Note: Camera surveillance not only has a preventive effect but also increases the chances of catching offenders. We consider infringement of privacy secondary here.

123688.explanation: List Babijn has previously proposed allowing permanent residency in recreational homes.

123689.explanation: We want to assess initiatives on their merits but do not want to promote 'ribbon development' like in Belgium.

123690.explanation: That increases the chance of a paid job.

123692.explanation: Everything is getting more expensive, so index according to national policy, but there is a limit to the financial capacity of our municipality.

123695.explanation: Adjust maximum speed only where necessary.

123696.explanation: With all the anticipated cutbacks, we should be grateful that any caregivers remain at all.

123699.explanation: Facilities for dogs must also be funded.

123703.explanation: However, this depends on national legislation.

123705.explanation: Agricultural land is intended for food production.

123709.explanation: Is part of our cultural heritage that needs attention.

123715.explanation: That would be the death blow for the center of Sluis.

123718.explanation: We are partly dependent on market forces and housing corporations.

123719.explanation: However, we strive for a good balance and want to focus on limited growth of the number of residents.

123660.explanation: Provided this has an advisory character and the democratically elected bodies make the decisions.

123663.explanation: Tourism should not be expanded further.

123664.explanation: It is essential to make the energy transition accessible and small-scale as well.

123665.explanation: Distribution centers create hardly any employment but do cause a lot of traffic nuisance. These large boxes spoil the landscape.

123667.explanation: All types of (temporary) workers should be housed just as decently as other citizens. The municipality is responsible for the control.

123670.explanation: A nonsensical way to use energy.

123672.explanation: Provided the number of collection rounds is limited to, for example, twice a year.

123674.explanation: Entrepreneurs must make their buildings accessible for people with disabilities using their own resources.

123675.explanation: Building in the countryside must meet criteria related to landscape and agricultural interests.

123677.explanation: The local broadcaster should be enabled to provide ample information and media literacy.

123679.explanation: In addition to music lessons, all children must be given ample opportunity to receive all forms of cultural education.

123682.explanation: Especially during the tourist season, this service must be adequately staffed.

123683.explanation: Social control can still be relied upon in Sluis, but cameras can have a function in busy shopping areas. Privacy laws also set necessary limits on camera surveillance. Moreover, more cameras do not automatically mean more security.

123688.explanation: Disagree, if this refers to housing on campsites and holiday parks.

123689.explanation: The natural and agricultural values must be respected.

123690.explanation: The supporting agencies must provide tailored support for those who still have prospects in volunteer or paid work.

123692.explanation: This limit is established by law.

123694.explanation: To combat the use of fossil energy, more charging points should be installed.

123695.explanation: For safety and the environment, this is a good measure.

123699.explanation: GroenLinks considers the tax of €65.00 per year acceptable.

123701.explanation: There is sufficient space on existing sites.

123703.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit are status holders and legally entitled to priority treatment.

123705.explanation: Disagree, provided the solar panels are part of an innovative, multiple-use concept.

123709.explanation: Besides proper Dutch, Zeeuws-Vlaams is valuable in our schools as part of our culture.

123710.explanation: As long as such a facility remains within the framework of the law.

123712.explanation: Provided this form of rental complies with municipal regulations.

123715.explanation: For safety and livability, GroenLinks supports this.

123718.explanation: The municipality must ensure there are sufficient homes in the social and mid-range rental sectors.

123719.explanation: In favor, but not at any cost.

123660.explanation: Residents must have direct influence on important decisions via binding referenda.

123663.explanation: Freedom of entrepreneurship and the right to use one’s own land. Especially in the case of farm shops. In the case of a campsite, tailored solutions must be provided.

123664.explanation: The municipality should not subsidize ideological climate policies; energy choice must be voluntary.

123665.explanation: Large distribution centers harm the landscape, livability, and infrastructure.

123667.explanation: Employers are responsible for housing seasonal workers. Employers must provide housing on their own premises.

123670.explanation: Entrepreneurs must not be restricted by symbolic policies.

123672.explanation: We already pay enough waste disposal fees. Free bulky waste collection contributes to proper processing. Not everyone can transport bulky waste themselves.

123674.explanation: The responsibility lies with the entrepreneur themselves.

123675.explanation: Farmers must have space to live and operate on their own land. But this must be proportional, always with tailored solutions and local considerations. Agricultural land is in principle not building land.

123679.explanation: Music lessons contribute to children's development. The municipality can encourage this where possible but should not make it mandatory. Schools and parents retain their own responsibility in this.

123682.explanation: Enforcement is necessary, but BOAs (special enforcement officers) are not a replacement for the police.

123683.explanation: Only targeted and temporary, if it proves necessary. Not structural.

123688.explanation: Sluis is primarily a residential municipality. In case of serious housing shortage, tailored solutions should be possible to protect and house residents. At the same time, permanent residence in holiday homes is not a structural solution. Therefore, no generic policy, only in cases of emergency.

123689.explanation: Limited building may be necessary on the edges of settlements, provided it suits the scale, environment, and demand.

123690.explanation: Participating according to one's abilities strengthens self-esteem and is beneficial for society.

123692.explanation: Assistance should be focused on residents who really need help. Raising the income threshold makes aid too general and less targeted. Prefer tailored solutions over automatically raising the income limit.

123694.explanation: Electric driving should not be forced. Those who choose to drive electric must take responsibility for charging the battery themselves.

123695.explanation: No generic speed reductions without proven necessity.

123699.explanation: Dog ownership should not be additionally taxed.

123705.explanation: Agricultural land is for food production.

123709.explanation: The Zeelandic-Flemish dialect is part of our regional identity. Schools should be given the space to pay attention to it without obligation or coercion.

123710.explanation: This carries risks, is a risk factor for safety and livability. This should not be facilitated.

123712.explanation: Rooming (dividing homes into multiple rental units) leads to more residents per dwelling who do not belong to one family. This causes increased parking pressure, noise nuisance, and less social cohesion in the neighborhood. Furthermore, these family homes disappear from the market.

123715.explanation: A car-free center makes shops and hospitality less accessible to residents and visitors. This may lead to fewer visitors and harms the liveliness and economy of Sluis.

123719.explanation: Growth for the sake of growth leads to pressure on space, housing, and facilities. Sluis must choose quality and livability, not maximum numbers.

123660.explanation: Insight into public opinion is important, but a referendum does not reach all layers of the population and is not representative of the opinions of all residents.

123663.explanation: We value short supply chains for food security. Giving farmers the chance to sell products through a farm shop promotes buying from local businesses.

123664.explanation: Subsidies for alternative energy sources can be requested from the government. This is not a task for our municipality.

123665.explanation: This requires a lot of space, generates a lot of traffic, and offers few jobs.

123667.explanation: This is already largely provided on a large scale by entrepreneurs in these sectors in our municipality. This works satisfactorily for both entrepreneurs and workers.

123672.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping in rural areas and roadside verges. Sluis offers the possibility to dispose of items free of charge at the Recycling Center or to have them collected at home by appointment with ZRD.

123674.explanation: Entrepreneurs who have to meet high requirements imposed by the national government should be able to apply for subsidies for this.

123675.explanation: A residential dwelling can conflict with agricultural activities.

123679.explanation: Music is important for children, but the responsibility lies with parents in upbringing.

123682.explanation: We have no signals from our residents that they experience insecurity. More police are desirable but we have no influence over this.

123688.explanation: In the municipality of Sluis, many homes may not be permanently occupied while we have a housing shortage.

123689.explanation: We support transformation of vacant buildings in our municipality but adding a street adjacent to the core should also be possible.

123690.explanation: Residents on welfare who can do socially meaningful work are valuable. Those who cannot have the opportunity to participate in other ways in our municipality.

123692.explanation: Better to provide timely assistance than to let residents deteriorate to the point of falling apart.

123694.explanation: This is already well arranged in our municipality.

123695.explanation: The current situation causes no problems and we see no need for a speed reduction.

123701.explanation: Expansion of existing industrial areas should be possible if there is demand.

123703.explanation: BBB Sluis believes that status holders should not get priority for housing. Given the limited size of the municipality, there is no room for reception.

123705.explanation: We do allow solar panels on roofs.

123709.explanation: We must preserve our uniqueness and dialect.

123712.explanation: The housing problem partly lies with the government but on a small scale this is already allowed in the recreational sector in our municipality.

123715.explanation: In the municipality of Sluis, the center is accessible to suppliers in the morning and on busy days the center is closed and car-free. Fully car-free is not necessary and unfeasible.

123718.explanation: National regulations are decisive, but we consider having sufficient housing very important.

statements.title: In new developments, 40% of the homes should be reserved for social and mid-range rental.
Residents of Sluis should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Sluis moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Sluis should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Sluis.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should enable all primary schools to include attention to the Zeelandic‑Flemish (Zeeuws‑Vlaams) dialect.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in the municipality of Sluis.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The center of Sluis should become car-free.
The municipality should aim to increase the number of residents.
